content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission

January 3, 1995

Renee Westa-Lusk
Chair 34th Assembly
District Democratic Committee
405 Montefino
Ridgecrest, CA 93555

Re: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. A-94-387

Dear Ms. Westa-Lusk:

You have requested advice concerning the campaign provisions
for the Political Reform Act (the "Act").1

QUESTIONS

1. The 34th Assembly District Democratic Committee (34th
ADDC) has appointed a new treasurer. The former treasurer refuses
to relinquish the committee's records. What are the committee's
options with regard to reporting the activities which occurred
under the former treasurer?

2. May people involved with the 34th ADDC form a new
committee and report just the activity of the new committee?

3. If a new committee is formed and $1,000 or more is
transferred from the old committee (34th ADDC) to the new
committee, what is the date of qualification of the new committee?

4. If a new committee is formed, is it necessary for the
existing 34th ADDC to be terminated and who would be responsible
for this?

5. If the former treasurer of the 34th ADDC refuses to file
a termination, who may be held responsible?

6. What are the consequences for not terminating a
committee that should be terminated?

_________

1 Government Code Sections 81000-91015. All statutory
references are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated.
Commission regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations
Section 18000, et seq. All references to regulations are to Title
2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations.

428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento, CA 95804-0807 • (916) 322-5660

Westa-Lusk, Renee
Our File No. A-94-387
Page 2


        7.    If the committee was obligated to file a pre-election
statement for 1994 which was not filed by the former treasurer,
will the old or new treasurer be responsible?

                            CONCLUSIONS

        1.    Committees may at any time appoint a new treasurer and
then must amend their Statement of Organization (Form 410) to
reflect any changes.  The treasurer is responsible for the
accuracy and completeness of any statements pertaining activity
occurring during the period when he or she is serving as
treasurer.  If a former treasurer is not cooperating in putting
together a report for a period during which he or she was the
treasurer, the current treasurer should reconstruct as much of the
information as possible, file any past due statements and include
a letter with the statements explaining the problems.

        2.    Anyone may form a committee and begin filing statements
reflecting the activity of the new committee.  However, this does
not relieve the organization of any obligation to ensure that the
existing committee is in compliance with the Act.

        3.    The date of qualification for any newly formed committee
is the date the committee receives contributions totaling $1,000.

        4.,5.& 6.   The law does not require that any committee be
terminated.  However, committees have ongoing campaign filing
obligations until they file a Statement of Termination (Form 415).

        7.    Treasurers are responsible for filing statements
required under the law.  Treasurers are also required to insure
the completeness and accuracy of any statements due for any
reporting periods while they hold the office of treasurer.
However, if a former treasurer is not cooperative, there is
nothing in the law which would prevent the current treasurer from
filing a past due statement for a period during which they were
not the treasurer.

                              FACTS

        You are the chairperson of the 34th ADDC.  The committee
recently designated a new person as treasurer and you indicated
that the former treasurer is unwilling to turn his records over to
the new treasurer.  You also indicated that you were not certain
if the 34th ADDC had enough activity to have incurred a filing
obligation for the pre-election statements for the November 8,
1994, election but that you thought it was likely and this would
have occurred within the tenure of the previous treasurer.  In
addition, you stated that you were not certain if the former
treasurer for the 34th ADDC has filed pre-election statements for
the November 1994 General Election.

Westa-Lusk, Renee
Our File No. A-94-387
Page 3


ANALYSIS

Section 82013(a) indicates that "committee" means any person
or combination of persons who directly or indirectly receives
contributions totaling one thousand dollars ($1,000) or more in a
calendar year.

Section 84101(a) states that every committee which is a
committee by virtue of subdivision (a) of Section 82013 shall file
with the Secretary of State a statement of organization within 10
days after it has qualified as a committee.

A committee may at any time for any reason appoint a new
treasurer. Any changes to a Statement of Organization must be
reported on an amended Form 410 which Government Code Section
84103 requires to be filed within 10 days of the effective date of
the change. The treasurer is responsible for the accuracy and
completeness of any statements for the time period during he or
she was serving as treasurer.

Section 81004 requires that all reports and statements filed
under the Political Reform Act be signed under penalty of perjury
and a recipient committee's statement must be signed and verified
by the committee's treasurer. The verification shall state that
the filer has used all reasonable diligence in its preparation,
and that to the best of his knowledge it is true and complete.
Therefore, a treasurer is responsible for the accuracy and
completeness of any statements reflecting activity occurring
during the period when he or she served as treasurer. If a former
treasurer is not cooperating in putting together a statement, the
current treasurer should recombiny the activity to the best of
his or her ability, file any past due statements and provide a
written explanation for any omissions or problems encountered in
preparing the statement.

We do not have much written advice regarding the legal
responsibilities of treasurers. However, we advised a former
treasurer that he was legally responsible for ensuring the
accuracy of a campaign statement covering a time period during
which he was treasurer, even though he did not sign that
statement. (Willems Advice Letter, No. A-91-128, copy enclosed.)

If you have additional questions, please contact me at (916)
322-5662.

Sincerely,

Steven G. Churchwell
General Counsel

By: Wayne P. Imberi
Political Reform Consultant

will the old or new treasurer be responsible?
